engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru WRZ Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru WRZ in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru WRZ EJ205 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ringland failure on pistons - common on high-mileage turbo units requiring full rebuild.
Head gasket leaks - known weakness on EJ205 boxer engines at higher mileage.
Turbocharger wear - VF series turbos can fail causing boost loss and oil consumption.

cards.altText: Subaru WRZ EJ207 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Piston ringland failure - notorious issue on EJ207 under hard driving conditions.
Bottom end bearing wear - spun bearings common on high-revving STI applications.
Turbo actuator faults - IHI VF turbos can develop boost control issues.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,895-£3,450 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EJ207 units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru WRZ EJ255 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ringland piston failure - most common catastrophic failure on EJ255 turbo engines.
Head gasket deterioration - EJ25 series prone to gasket leaks at high mileage.
Oil consumption issues - worn piston rings cause blue smoke and frequent top-ups.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru WRZ Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru WRZ's turbocharged boxer engines are powerful but can suffer from known weaknesses. Ringland failure on EJ255/257 and head gasket issues on EJ20 series dominate replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Ringland Piston Failure (EJ255/257) - Why WRZ Turbo Engines Lose Compression
problems.affectedModels: Subaru WRZ WRX EJ255, WRX STI EJ257 (2006-2014, turbo petrol)
problems.rootCause: High cylinder pressures and heat cause piston ring lands to crack, allowing combustion gases to escape into the crankcase.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Piston Ring Replacement - Partial rebuild
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace piston rings, hone bores, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: May extend life 20-40k miles if block is undamaged; not suitable if cylinders scored

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Short Block Replacement - Bottom end rebuild
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crankshaft, pistons, rods, bearings; reuse cylinder heads if undamaged
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if heads are good; addresses root cause of ringland failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt EJ255/257 with upgraded pistons, rings, bearings, gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; includes 12-month warranty, upgraded components prevent recurrence

problems.recommendation: On a WRZ worth £3,000-£8,000, full engine replacement with upgraded pistons offers better long-term value than partial repairs. Ringland failure often indicates broader bottom-end wear.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ255 EJ257 engine prices for your Subaru WRZ

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure (EJ20 Series) - Why WRZ Boxer Engines Leak Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Subaru WRZ WRX EJ205, EJ207 (2000-2007, turbo petrol)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling and combustion pressure cause multi-layer steel gaskets to degrade, allowing coolant-oil mixing or external leaks.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Head Gasket Replacement - Seal repair only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace gaskets, resurface heads if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: May last 30-50k miles if heads are flat; doesn't address underlying wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt EJ205/207 with upgraded MLS gaskets, new studs, refreshed components
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; upgraded gaskets and studs prevent recurrence, includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your WRZ has over 90k miles or shows other wear signs, replacement with upgraded gaskets is more economical than repeated gasket repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ205 EJ207 engine prices for your Subaru WRZ

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why WRZ Boost Pressure Drops
problems.affectedModels: Subaru WRZ WRX/STI all turbo variants (2000-2014, EJ205/207/255/257)
problems.rootCause: High exhaust temperatures and oil starvation cause turbo bearing wear, shaft play, and eventual seizure or boost loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Rebuild/Replacement - Turbo unit only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old turbo, fit rebuilt or replacement VF-series turbo, reconnect oil/coolant lines
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost if engine is healthy; doesn't address potential engine damage from turbo failure debris

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt turbo boxer with new turbo, upgraded oil feed, refreshed internals
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution if turbo failure caused engine damage; prevents recurrence, includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure caused oil contamination or debris entered the engine, full replacement is safer than turbo-only repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt turbo boxer engine prices for your Subaru WRZ

problems.h4: Bottom End Bearing Failure - Why WRZ Engines Develop Knocking
problems.affectedModels: Subaru WRZ WRX/STI high-mileage turbo variants (all EJ series)
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation, high RPM use, or worn oil pumps cause bearing wear, leading to rod knock and potential catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Bottom End Rebuild - Bearing replacement only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Split engine block, replace bearings, inspect crank/rods, reassemble with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if crank and rods are undamaged; requires expert machining and assembly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt boxer with new bearings, upgraded oil pump, refreshed components
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; addresses root causes, includes 12-month warranty, prevents recurrence

problems.recommendation: Bearing knock indicates advanced wear - full replacement with upgraded components offers better long-term reliability than partial rebuilds.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt boxer engine prices for your Subaru WRZ

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru WRZ
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're experiencing ringland failure, head gasket leaks, turbo issues, or bearing knock, EngineMarket connects you with UK Subaru turbo boxer specialists who understand WRZ engines. Compare prices from vetted suppliers, all offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components and 12-month unlimited mileage warranties. Get back on the road with confidence - without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru WRZ Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru WRZ and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru WRZ Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Subaru WRZ is listed, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

items.title: Subaru WRZ 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L turbo boxer defined the WRZ's performance heritage, powering classic GC8 and GD chassis WRX/STI models. EJ205 and EJ207 codes deliver 218-280hp with distinctive turbo boxer character. Ringland failure and head gasket issues are the most common replacement triggers.
items.cta: Compare Subaru WRZ 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru WRZ 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The larger 2.5L turbo boxer brought increased torque to later WRZ models from 2006 onwards. EJ255 and EJ257 codes power WRX and STI variants with 225-300hp, but suffer more frequently from ringland piston failure than the 2.0L units.
items.cta: Compare Subaru WRZ 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.familiesLabel: Common Subaru WRZ Petrol Engine Families
families: EJ205 - 2.0L turbo WRX (218-230hp), most common UK replacement code
EJ207 - 2.0L turbo STI (265-280hp), JDM-spec high-revving performance unit
EJ255 - 2.5L turbo WRX (225-265hp), later model with more torque
EJ257 - 2.5L turbo STI (300hp), ultimate performance boxer code
foundIn: GC8 chassis (1992-2000) - early WRX/STI with EJ20G/EJ20K
GD chassis (2000-2007) - blobeye/hawkeye WRX/STI with EJ205/EJ207
GV/GF chassis (2008-2014) - hatchback/saloon WRX/STI with EJ255/EJ257
knownFor: Distinctive turbo boxer sound and AWD traction

Head gasket issues on EJ20 series at higher mileage
Strong enthusiast community and parts availability

engineYears.h2: Subaru WRZ Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Subaru WRZ production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The GD chassis defined the modern WRZ with EJ205 (WRX) and EJ207 (STI) turbo boxer engines.
keyChanges: Introduction of GD chassis replacing classic GC8
EJ205 turbo boxer standard on WRX (218-230hp)
EJ207 JDM-spec boxer on STI imports (265-280hp)
Symmetrical AWD standard across all variants
knownFor: Ringland failure emerging on high-mileage EJ205 units
Strong aftermarket support and tuning potential

faq.h2: Subaru WRZ Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru WRZ engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru WRZ engine replacement costs typically range from £895 for used units to £3,950+ for reconditioned turbo boxer engines, depending on engine code and warranty level.
keyPoints: Used EJ205/EJ255 engines start around £895-£1,495 supply only
Reconditioned turbo boxer engines range £1,495-£3,950 depending on code
Supply & fit labour adds £495-£895 at approved Subaru specialists
STI-spec EJ207/EJ257 codes command premium pricing due to performance demand
items.cta: Compare Subaru WRZ engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit the Subaru WRZ?
items.answer: The Subaru WRZ uses EJ-series turbo boxer engines: EJ205, EJ207, EJ255 and EJ257 are the primary codes, with EJ20G/EJ20K on classic GC8 models.
keyPoints: EJ207: 2.0L turbo STI (265-280hp), JDM-spec high-revving unit
EJ257: 2.5L turbo STI (300hp), ultimate performance code
items.cta: Check which engine code fits your Subaru WRZ

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru Legacy or Forester engine in my WRZ?
items.answer: While Legacy and Forester share EJ-series boxer architecture, WRZ turbo engines have unique ECU mapping, turbo systems and AWD calibration - direct swaps require specialist adaptation.
keyPoints: EJ20/EJ25 blocks are physically compatible across Subaru models
Turbo WRZ engines need matching turbo, intercooler and ECU calibration
AWD differential ratios and centre diff calibration must match WRZ spec
Specialist coding required for immobiliser and drive-by-wire systems
items.cta: Get quotes for WRZ-compatible boxer engine replacement

items.question: How long does a reconditioned Subaru WRZ boxer engine last?
items.answer: A properly reconditioned Subaru WRZ boxer engine with upgraded components can last 80,000-120,000+ miles with regular maintenance and appropriate driving.
keyPoints: Upgraded pistons and rings address original ringland failure weaknesses
Multi-layer steel gaskets and torque-to-yield studs improve head sealing
Regular oil changes with correct specification critical for turbo longevity
Avoiding excessive boost and ensuring proper warm-up extends engine life
items.cta: View reconditioned Subaru WRZ engine warranty options

items.question: Does a Subaru WRZ engine replacement include warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all Subaru WRZ engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with extended options available for performance variants.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on reconditioned units
Turbo components and gaskets covered under warranty terms
Extended 24-month options available for STI and high-performance builds
Warranty valid at any UK garage with proper service documentation
items.cta: Compare Subaru WRZ engine warranty terms and coverage

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Subaru WRZ boxer engine?
items.answer: A Subaru WRZ boxer engine replacement typically takes 1-2 days for supply and 1-2 days for fitting at a specialist garage, with ECU adaptation included.
keyPoints: Engine supply: 1-3 days for UK stock, 5-10 days for JDM import units
Fitting time: 8-16 hours labour depending on transmission and AWD complexity
ECU adaptation and immobiliser matching included in specialist fitting
Road test and compression verification completed before handover
items.cta: Book Subaru WRZ engine supply and fit with approved specialists

items.question: Do I need to reprogram the ECU after a Subaru WRZ engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, Subaru WRZ engine replacements require ECU adaptation for immobiliser matching, drive-by-wire calibration and AWD system verification - included with specialist fitting.
keyPoints: Immobiliser transponder must be matched to new engine ECU
Drive-by-wire throttle adaptation required for smooth operation
AWD centre diff calibration ensures correct torque distribution
Specialist Subaru diagnostic equipment required for full adaptation
items.cta: Get quotes for WRZ engine replacement with ECU coding included

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and remanufactured WRZ engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt WRZ engines address specific failures, reconditioned units undergo full strip and upgrade, remanufactured engines meet original Subaru specifications with certified documentation.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: Targeted repair of known failure points (rings, gaskets, turbo)
Reconditioned: Full strip, measure, machine and rebuild with upgraded components
Remanufactured: Factory-spec rebuild with OEM tolerances and certification
Warranty length and component quality increase with each tier
items.cta: Compare Subaru WRZ engine rebuild options and pricing

items.question: Can I drive my Subaru WRZ with a faulty boxer engine to your fitting centre?
items.answer: Driving a Subaru WRZ with severe engine faults risks catastrophic damage - we recommend recovery to a specialist garage for assessment before any replacement work begins.
keyPoints: Loss of oil pressure or severe knocking indicates imminent failure
Coolant-oil mixing from head gasket failure can destroy bearings quickly
Turbo failure debris can score cylinders beyond repair
Recovery to specialist assessment prevents further damage and cost
items.cta: Arrange Subaru WRZ engine recovery and specialist assessment

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Subaru WRZ WRX or STI?
items.answer: For WRZ values of £3,000-£12,000, engine replacement with upgraded components often preserves more value than scrapping, especially for STI and Prodrive variants.
keyPoints: STI and Prodrive WRZ models retain strong enthusiast demand
Reconditioned engines with upgrades address original failure points
Full service history and warranty support future resale value
Compare replacement cost against current market value of your specific variant
items.cta: Calculate if Subaru WRZ engine replacement is worthwhile for your vehicle
